title: Stand in solidarity with Minnesota
blurb: Mission, values, and how to get involved.

title: Our Values
blurb: Dignity, solidarity, subsidiarity, and the common good.

title: Events
blurb: Press conferences, marches, vigils, trainings.

title: Get Help
blurb: A living map of where to turn — legal, food, housing, health.

title: If a loved one was detained
blurb: Step-by-step in the first hours after detention — locator, lawyers, money on books.

title: Know Your Rights
blurb: Bilingual cards on what to say at the door, in a car stop, and at work.

title: News & Updates
blurb: Live ICE updates and immigration coverage from trusted outlets.

title: Stories
blurb: Real moments — what happens when a community stands together.

title: Coalition Partners
blurb: The organizations we stand with across MN.

title: Take Action
blurb: Volunteer, host a KYR night, or join a rapid-response team.

title: Court Support
blurb: Volunteer to accompany a neighbor to immigration court — a row of community in the gallery.

title: Donate
blurb: Funded by neighbors, spent on neighbors. Rent, food, legal aid.

title: Membership
blurb: Become a member of the movement.

title: Store
blurb: Yard signs, t-shirts, button packs.

title: Contact
blurb: Email, phone, hotline, in-person hours.

title: Volunteer Lawyers Network — walk-in legal clinic
blurb: Free legal advice for low-income Minnesotans on housing, family, debt, and immigration referrals.
keywords: Legal aid 600 Nicollet Mall, Minneapolis, MN 612-752-6677 English Spanish Somali

title: Immigrant Law Center of Minnesota
blurb: Direct immigration legal services and Know-Your-Rights trainings statewide.
keywords: Legal aid 450 N Syndicate St, St. Paul, MN 651-641-1011 English Spanish French Karen

title: Mid-Minnesota Legal Aid
blurb: Free civil legal help for low-income Minnesotans — housing, family, public benefits, disability.
keywords: Legal aid 111 N 5th St, Suite 100, Minneapolis, MN 612-334-5970 English Spanish

title: The Advocates for Human Rights
blurb: Pro bono asylum, U-visa, and VAWA representation. Country-conditions research support.
keywords: Legal aid 330 2nd Ave S, Suite 800, Minneapolis, MN 612-341-3302

title: Southern Minnesota Regional Legal Services
blurb: Civil legal aid covering Ramsey County and southern MN — housing, benefits, family law.
keywords: Legal aid 55 E 5th St, St. Paul, MN 651-222-5863

title: CAIR Minnesota — civil rights
blurb: Civil rights and discrimination support for Muslim community members. Hate-crime intake.

title: Loaves & Fishes — free hot meals
blurb: Hot dinners served nightly at multiple Twin Cities locations — no questions asked.

title: Second Harvest Heartland — food shelf locator
blurb: Find a food shelf or mobile pantry near you. Multilingual phone help available.

title: Community Emergency Service — Phillips food shelf
blurb: Choice-style food shelf and emergency assistance for South Minneapolis neighbors.
keywords: Food 1900 11th Ave S, Minneapolis, MN 612-870-1125 English Spanish Somali

title: Sabathani Community Center — food shelf
blurb: Choice food shelf serving South Minneapolis. Also offers senior services and youth programs.

title: Keystone Community Services — food shelf
blurb: Three food shelf locations across St. Paul. Walk-in welcome; no documents required.
keywords: Food 1916 University Ave W, St. Paul, MN 651-645-0349

title: Hallie Q. Brown Community Center — food shelf
blurb: Food shelf serving the Rondo and Summit-University neighborhoods of St. Paul.
keywords: Food 270 N Kent St, St. Paul, MN 651-224-4601

title: Open Arms of Minnesota
blurb: Free, medically-tailored meal delivery for people living with serious illness.

title: Sharing & Caring Hands / Mary's Place
blurb: Free meals daily, clothing, hygiene, and transitional shelter near downtown Minneapolis.

title: HOME Line — tenant rights hotline
blurb: Free tenant rights advice for renters in Minnesota. Eviction defense help.

title: Neighborhood House — basic needs & ESL
blurb: Food shelf, ESL classes, citizenship help, basic-needs case management.
keywords: Housing 179 Robie St E, St. Paul, MN 651-789-2500

title: People Serving People — family shelter
blurb: Largest family-focused emergency shelter in Minnesota. Children's programming on site.

title: Catholic Charities — Dorothy Day Place
blurb: Emergency shelter, day services, and supportive housing in downtown St. Paul.
keywords: Housing 411 Main St, St. Paul, MN 651-647-2350

title: Simpson Housing Services
blurb: Shelter and supportive housing across the Twin Cities, with specific family programs.

title: House of Charity
blurb: Daily free lunch and supportive housing for people experiencing homelessness in Minneapolis.

title: Hennepin Healthcare — Indigent Care
blurb: Sliding-scale clinic visits regardless of immigration status or insurance.

title: People's Center Health Services
blurb: Federally-qualified community health center serving Cedar-Riverside and beyond.
keywords: Health 425 20th Ave S, Minneapolis, MN 612-332-4973 English Somali Oromo Spanish

title: NorthPoint Health & Wellness Center
blurb: Comprehensive medical, dental, and behavioral health for North Minneapolis families.
keywords: Health 1313 Penn Ave N, Minneapolis, MN 612-543-2500

title: Native American Community Clinic
blurb: Primary care, behavioral health, and traditional healing for Native and all communities.
keywords: Health 1213 E Franklin Ave, Minneapolis, MN 612-872-8086

title: Open Cities Health Center
blurb: Sliding-scale primary care, dental, and behavioral health serving St. Paul.
keywords: Health 409 N Dunlap St, St. Paul, MN 651-290-9200

title: West Side Community Health Services / La Clínica
blurb: Bilingual primary care for the West Side of St. Paul. Sliding scale, all welcome.
keywords: Health 153 Cesar Chavez St, St. Paul, MN 651-602-7500 English Spanish

title: Indian Health Board of Minneapolis
blurb: Comprehensive health services with traditional Native practices, open to all.

title: MN Interfaith Coalition on Immigration
blurb: A coalition of faith communities offering accompaniment, sanctuary, and pastoral support.

title: St. Frances Cabrini Catholic Church
blurb: Sanctuary-supportive parish in the Prospect Park neighborhood. Multilingual community.
keywords: Sanctuary 1500 Franklin Ave SE, Minneapolis, MN 612-378-9300

title: Plymouth Congregational Church
blurb: Open-and-affirming UCC congregation engaged in immigrant accompaniment.

title: Holy Trinity Lutheran Church
blurb: Sanctuary-supportive ELCA congregation in the Longfellow neighborhood.

title: Know-Your-Rights workshops (rotating)
blurb: Free monthly trainings on what to do at the door, in a car stop, and at work.

title: CTUL — Centro de Trabajadores Unidos en Lucha
blurb: Worker-led organizing for low-wage workers. Wage-theft clinics and KYR trainings.
keywords: Know your rights 3715 Chicago Ave, Minneapolis, MN 612-876-9263 English Spanish

title: COPAL Minnesota
blurb: Latinx-led racial-justice organization. Workers' rights, immigration, and civic engagement.
keywords: Know your rights 850 W 38th St, Minneapolis, MN 612-355-7448 English Spanish

title: ISAIAH Minnesota
blurb: Multiracial faith-based coalition organizing for racial and economic justice.

title: MIRAC Rapid Response Network
blurb: 24-hour hotline to verify ICE activity and dispatch trained observers across MN.

title: Asamblea de Derechos Civiles
blurb: Grassroots immigrant rights organizing and rapid-response coordination.

title: Press Conference: Extend Temporary Protective Status
blurb: A press conference calling on Congress and the administration to extend Temporary Protective Status for affected Minnesotans. Coalition partners, faith communities, and TPS holders will speak.
keywords: Stand in Solidarity · Minnesota State Capitol Immigrant-Led · Minnesota-Strong · Stand in Solidarity Minnesota State Capitol — Press Conference Room B971 St. Paul, MN 55155

title: March For Immigrants
blurb: A community march from Uptown to demand the extension of Temporary Protective Status. Bring family, banners, and water — we walk together.
keywords: Extend Temporary Protective Status Immigrant-Led · Minnesota-Strong · Stand in Solidarity Hennepin Ave. & W. Lake St. Minneapolis, MN

title: Keeping Families Home — rental & utility relief
blurb: Direct support for families facing eviction or shut-offs after a sudden loss of income or change in status.

title: Food on the Table — immediate food relief
blurb: Groceries delivered and hot meals shared with neighbors who are stretched thin between paychecks, hours, and red tape.

title: Legal Resources — legal navigation
blurb: Help finding the right immigration, housing, or family law attorney — and someone to walk with you to court.
